content.full_text:
Americans Against Human Suffering inc.
2505 Canada Boulevard
Suite 1B
Glendale, CA 91208

P.O. Box 11001
Glendale, CA 91206
(818) 240 1986

Freedom of Choice for Physician Aid-in-Dying

325 Pennsylvania Ave., SE
Washington, DC 20003
(202) 223 8633

February 7, 1990

Jeanne Pritchard
Division Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807


Dear Ms. Pritchard:

Americans Against Human Suffering, initiative qualification
committee, #862473 needs further clarification regarding its
responsibilities to file contribution and expenditure reports
with the Secretary of State's Office.

We received conflicting advice over the telephone on these
questions and would appreciate a written opinion.

Our committee attempted to qualify an initiative for the
November 1988 ballot but failed. We filed a summary report
for the period January 1, 1986-June 6, 1988. We were
notified in writing by the Secretary of State's office that
we needed to file a supplementary report for the period June
7-June 30, 1988. However, subsequently we were notified that
we were not required to file a report until the initiative
qualifies or fails to qualify (please see the enclosed
letters).

We continued to collect funds and make expenditures in 1988
and 1989 anticipating an attempt to qualify our measure for a
later ballot.

We are unclear about our ongoing obligations to file reports.

Are we required to file semi-annual reports in the
period between our attempts to qualify an initiative?

ADVISORY BOARD
DIRECTORS                          Richard Dreyfuss              MEDICAL ADVISORY BOARD
Robert L. Bailey, J.D.            Betty Rollin                Allan K. Britney, M.D.
  Chairman                         Diane Wickes, MSW           Steven K. Yarnell, M.D.
Lloyd Eggers, J.D.                Ruth Bertling               Thomas M. Brod, M.D.        LEGAL ADVISORY BOARD
Ann Wickett, Ph.D.                Doris Davis, Ph.D.          Richard B Doering, M.D.     Richard Scott, J.D.
William Robison                   George Sayer, USNR          Bernhard Penner, M.D.       Michael H. White, J.D.
                                  Robert L. King, DDS         Howard Caplan, M.D.         J. Harold Williams, J.D., M.D.
OFFICERS                          Bruno Bettelheim, Ph.D.
Michael H. White, J.D.            Michael H. White, J.D.
President                         Alan Johnson, Ph.D.

Jeanne Pritchard
Division Chief
February 7, 1990
Page 2

If not, does that mean that the June 7-June 30, 1988
report requested by the Secretary of State's office is
unnecessary?

If we are only required to file a summary report at the
conclusion of our next qualification effort, does that
ruling contemplate the nearly four year time span in our
case. June '88 to June '92?

In this four year report, do we follow the same rules as
in other reports? For example, shall we provide
cumulative totals for givers on a calendar year basis,
or do we provide cumulative numbers for all contributors
for the full four years?

We were planning to file the reports for the periods from
June 7, 1988 to December 31, 1989 by January 31, 1990 until
we received the conflicting advise and were advised not to
file at this time. Please give us as precise direction as
possible in this matter as soon as possible.

Very truly yours,

AMERICANS AGAINST HUMAN SUFFERING, INC.

By MARY LOLONIS,
Secretary

ML:gt
ltr.114

2506 Canals Boulevard
Suite 2
Glendale, CA 91208

Americans Against Human Suffering INC.
Freedom of Choice for Physician Aid-in-Dying

P.O. Box 11001
Glendale, CA 91206

October 3, 1988

Secretary of State
Political Reform Division
P.O. Box 1467
1230 J Street, Room 219
Sacramento, CA 95812-1467

Re: Americans Against Human Suffering, Inc.
    ID #862473

Dear Madam:

Pursuant to the Secretary of State's letter of August 25, 1988, we have amended our Campaign Disclosure Statement. Enclosed please find original and one copy of Form 405 Amendment to Campaign Disclosure Statement for the above-referenced organization.

Please be advised that Harvey Tsuboi of the Secretary of State's Office granted a two-month extension of time within which to file the Amendment. Please stamp the copy of this letter and return it in the pre-addressed stamped envelope. Should you have any questions or comments, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly yours,

AMERICANS AGAINST HUMAN SUFFERING

By MARY LOLONIS
   Assistant to the President
   MLlgt
   encl.
   ltr.028

OFFICERS
Robert L. Ridley, J.D.
President
William Robson
Treasurer/CEO

DIRECTORS
Boyd Reuben, LL.B
Ann Wickes, Ph.D
William Robson
Michael H. White, J.D
Alan Johnson, Ph.D

ADVISORY BOARD
Richard Dreyfuss
Berry Rollin
Diane Wickes, MSW
Ruth Bending
Doris Davis, Ph.D
George Soros, USNR
Robert L. King, DDS
Bruno Bettelheim, Ph.D

MEDICAL ADVISORY BOARD
M. D. Brown, M.D
Steven K. Terrell, M.D
Thomas M. Boyd, M.D
Richard B. Doering, M.D
Bernhard Penner, M.D
Howard Caplan, M.D

LEGAL ADVISORY BOARD
Richard Scott, J.D, M.D
Michael H. White, J.D
J. Harold Williams, J.D, M.D

embedding.qa_text: February 7, 1990 Jeanne Pritchard Division Chief Technical Assistance and Analysis Division Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 Dear Ms. Pritchard: Americans Against Human Suffering, initiative qualification committee, #862473 needs further clarification regarding its responsibilities to file contribution and expenditure reports with the Secretary of State’s Office. We received conflicting advice over the telephone on these questions and would appreciate a written opinion. Our committee attempted to qualify an initiative for the November 1988 ballot but failed. We filed a summary report for the period January 1, 1986-June 6, 1988. We were notified in writing by the Secretary of State’s office that we needed to file a supplementary report for the period June 7-June 30, 1988. However, subsequently we were notified that we were not required to file a report until the initiative qualifies or fails to qualify (please see the enclosed letters). We continued to collect funds and make expenditures in 1988 and 1989 anticipating an attempt to qualify our measure for a later ballot. We are unclear about our ongoing obligations to file reports. Are we required to file semi-annual reports in the period between our attempts to qualify an initiative? Jeanne Pritchard Division Chief February 7, 1990 Page 2 If not, does that mean that the June 7-June 30, 1988 report requested by the Secretary of State's office is unnecessary? If we are only required to file a summary report at the conclusion of our next qualification effort, does that ruling contemplate the nearly four year time span in our case. June '88 to June '92? In this four year report, do we follow the same rules as in other reports? For example, shall we provide cumulative totals for givers on a calendar year basis, or do we provide cumulative numbers for all contributors for the full four years? We were planning to file the reports for the periods from June 7, 1988 to December 31, 1989 by January 31, 1990 until we received the conflicting advise and were advised not to file at this time. Please give us as precise direction as possible in this matter as soon as possible. Very truly yours, AMERICANS AGAINST HUMAN SUFFERING, INC. [signature] By MARY LOLONIS, Secretary ML:gt ltr.114 Below are Mr. Tsuboi's Comments. Two-months is the period I refer non-filers (amendments) to the Commission. Also, I called the FPPC to verify that you do not need to file any further statements till your initiative again qualifies to qualify in 1990.
